prompt: The floating-point comparison never passes. What is the bug?
choices: double cannot represent 0.3 at all; use float instead
0.1 + 0.2 produces 0.30000000000000004 due to floating-point representation errors; never compare doubles with ==
The + operator on double returns a String
0.3 is implicitly cast to int (0) before comparison
explanation: Floating-point arithmetic is not exact. 0.1 + 0.2 = 0.30000000000000004 in IEEE 754 double precision. Fix: use Math.abs(a - 0.3) < 1e-9 for epsilon comparison, or use BigDecimal for exact decimal arithmetic.
